description: Draft of the beginning of a letter in Judaeo-Arabic. The hand is typical to the 13th century, and is probably that of Shelomo b. Eliyyahu (compare with T-S NS 304.7). He is writing to the distinguished physician Abū ʿAlī and complains that he has been suffering from a fever of the liver (ḥummā al-kabid) for the last several months. On verso there are pen trials and jottings in Hebrew and Arabic script, probably also by Shelomo. AA/ASE
